target: SEC-2b.5: disambiguate dome-side WIN-NNN labels in Domain-by-domain audit from our review's WIN namespace
summary: SEC-2b.5's Domain-by-domain audit lists 14 static re-references, 4 Tesla-family rows, and a closing two-sentence inflation call-out, all of which use bare "WIN-NNN" labels copied from the dome's status_history.json source strings (e.g. "(WIN-001 static win)"). The WIN-NNN format is the same one our review uses for our own WIN namespace in every other section of the site (grep confirms 300+ occurrences of "WIN-NNN" in part1/part2/part2b/part4 etc., referring to entries in wins.json). Two concrete collisions sharpen the risk: (a) the dome's WIN-015 in that audit row is "SAA Decay Rate 77=77 nT/yr", but our WIN-015 in wins.json is "Meyl scalar Faraday" — the same label points at different claims; (b) the audit cites WIN-075 and WIN-076, neither of which exist in our wins.json (last primary ID is 069). A reader following a "WIN-015" link with our section conventions would be sent to the wrong claim. The fix is a surgical notation note plus one small wording tweak on the closing sentence — no rewrite, no repagination.

ambiguity_evidence: Our review uses 'WIN-NNN' prose labels across every major section (grep of data/sections.json: part1=49, part1b=14, part2=48, part2b=74, part4=28, part5=4, part6=43, part7=20, part8=6, part9=11, part10=3). Pattern is the canonical way we refer to entries in wins.json.
Dome's status_history.json source strings use the same 'WIN-NNN' format for its own internal WIN identifiers, captured verbatim in SEC-2b.5's Static re-references list (e.g. '(WIN-015 static win)').
Numerical collision at WIN-015: dome-side = 'SAA Decay Rate 77 nT/yr'; our wins.json id=015 = 'Meyl scalar Faraday'. Different claims, identical label.

Namespace overflow: dome cites WIN-075 (Ionospheric D-layer) and WIN-076 (Mascon Gravity) — neither exists in our wins.json (our last primary ID is 069). These labels are ONLY resolvable in the dome's numbering.
affected_locations_in_section:
Tesla-family ...

list — two rows tagged STATIC (WIN-001 static win) and STATIC (WIN-008 static win)

Static re-references to prior WINs (14 domains)

— 14-item
    listing dome-labels WIN-001, WIN-008, WIN-015, WIN-024 (twice), WIN-026, WIN-039 (twice), WIN-056, WIN-065, WIN-075, WIN-076, plus one OPEN-007
Closing paragraph after that <ol>: 'Critically, WIN-024 appears twice ... WIN-001, WIN-039 each appear twice.'
verification_report.why_notation_note_over_relabeling: Rewriting each bullet to say 'dome-WIN-NNN' inside source-string parentheticals would misquote the dome's own source strings (the dome's status_history.json literally says '(WIN-024 static win)', not '(dome-WIN-024 static win)'). A notation note placed once, near the top of the audit, lets the verbatim source-string quotations stand while eliminating the ambiguity for readers.

patches.location:
SEC-2b.5 > 'Domain-by-domain audit' intro, immediately after the categories
    and before the

    Tesla-family

    subsection
patches.current_text:
• DISCRIMINATING — a genuine numerical comparison with meaningful tolerance, not traceable to the categories above.

    • Tesla-family (driven by disc_radius = 20,015 km and κ = 1.672)
patches.replacement_text:
• DISCRIMINATING — a genuine numerical comparison with meaningful tolerance, not traceable to the categories above.

    • Notation: Throughout this audit, WIN-NNN labels inside source-string parentheticals and table references (e.g. "(WIN-001 static win)", "WIN-015", "WIN-075") are the dome site's own identifiers from its status_history.json — they are not references to entries in our review's WIN namespace. Our numbering and the dome's diverge in two places visible in this audit: (i) our WIN-015 is Meyl scalar Faraday while the dome's WIN-015 in this table is SAA Decay Rate, and (ii) the dome cites WIN-075 and WIN-076 (Ionospheric D-layer, Mascon Gravity), which sit past the last ID in our wins.json. When we need to point at one of our own WIN entries from this section we link or write "our WIN-NNN" explicitly.

    Tesla-family (driven by disc_radius = 20,015 km and κ = 1.672)
patches.rationale: Places the disambiguation once, in the location that covers every subsequent WIN-NNN reference in the audit, while preserving the dome's verbatim source-string quotations in the bullets. The notation names two concrete divergences so a reader who doubts the ambiguity has a check they can run. Closes the curmudgeon's ISS-1104 concern at zero argumentative cost — if anything the notation tightens the 'one ruler, many rooms' thesis by underlining that the dome has its own numbering we are faithfully reporting, not our WINs being replayed.

patches.location: SEC-2b.5 > 'Static re-references to prior WINs (14 domains)' > closing paragraph after the <ol>
patches.current_text: Critically, WIN-024 appears twice (status_history.json row indices 27 and 33 of the 39-row snapshot, corresponding to Roaring 40s Wind Speed and SAA–Roaring 40s Boundary); WIN-001, WIN-039 each appear twice. Counting a single fixed value as two independent "passes" is the purest form of label inflation.
patches.replacement_text: Critically, the dome's WIN-024 appears twice (status_history.json row indices 27 and 33 of the 39-row snapshot, corresponding to Roaring 40s Wind Speed and SAA–Roaring 40s Boundary); the dome's WIN-001 and WIN-039 each appear twice. Counting a single fixed value as two independent "passes" — and, in the case of WIN-024, counting its boundary as a separate domain from the wind speed that defines that same boundary — is the purest form of label inflation.
patches.rationale: The rhetorical crescendo of the section uses bare 'WIN-024', 'WIN-001', 'WIN-039' — readers who missed the notation upstream most need the reminder here. Adding 'the dome's' four times is minimal-touch and prevents a reader from navigating to our WIN-024 ('Roaring 40s = SAA boundary' — which happens to match the dome's topic but is our own entry) and concluding we were the ones double-counting. The small parenthetical on the boundary-vs-wind-speed double-count strengthens the inflation argument without changing its substance.

holes_addressed: ISS-1104 (moderate, citation): WIN labels in the SEC-2b.5 domain audit table use the same WIN-NNN format as our review's WIN namespace. Add 'dome-WIN-' prefix or parenthetical at list start to disambiguate. Addressed via P1 (notation paragraph covering the full audit) and P2 (inline disambiguation on the closing inflation sentence).
new_evidence_added: Two concrete collisions documented: our WIN-015 (Meyl scalar Faraday) vs dome's WIN-015 (SAA Decay Rate); dome's WIN-075/WIN-076 lie past our last primary WIN id (069).
Grep census of WIN-NNN references across data/sections.json confirms the label pattern is canonical throughout our review (300+ occurrences).

anticipated_objections.objection: Why not just prefix every audit-table label as 'dome-WIN-NNN'?
anticipated_objections.response: Because the dome's own source strings are literal quotations — '(WIN-001 static win)' comes straight from status_history.json. Relabeling them inside the quote would misquote a file we criticize the dome for drifting on. A notation note preserves the quotation integrity while closing the reader-ambiguity gap.

anticipated_objections.objection: Readers will figure out from context that these are dome labels.
anticipated_objections.response: Most will — but we already have two concrete collisions that a careful dome defender can weaponize (our WIN-015 ≠ their WIN-015; their WIN-075/076 don't exist in our corpus). One sentence up-front kills that attack surface. At the rhetorical crescendo of the section we also inline-mark the four most-cited labels because that is where a reader is most likely to click-through.

anticipated_objections.objection: This is a trivial citation fix — you're not doing real scientific work.
anticipated_objections.response: The analyst queue had no higher-priority work this run (Modes 0, 1, 1b, 2, 2b, 3, 4 all empty; changes_pending_analysis=0). ISS-1104 is assigned-analyst from the curmudgeon's SEC-2b.5 c1 review and blocks issue closure. Citation precision is part of the standard — EXP-175 just landed for the same reason on the Timestamp Error tab.

kernel_of_truth: The dome author's decision to re-use 'WIN-NNN' formatting for his own internal status_history.json source strings is a genuine act of internal consistency — he uses one label namespace for his onboarding pipeline. The curmudgeon's catch is that our review re-uses the exact same format for a different namespace, so when we quote his source strings inside our prose the two namespaces collide. The fix is not to criticize his naming — it's to name the collision for the reader. That is scientific hygiene, and doing it cleanly (one notation note + four minimal in-prose 'the dome's' prefixes) protects the integrity of the rest of the audit.
tldr_patch_note: The SEC-2b.5 TLDR ('28/28 scored domains passing at 100.0%' ... 'roughly six dome parameters, fourteen static re-references to prior WINs, and a handful of values literally borrowed from the globe model') does not mention specific WIN-NNN labels and does not need a patch. Part 4.1 p4-41-0 TLDR was updated in EXP-176 and similarly does not cite specific dome-WIN labels.
